mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 6 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and eases financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of level.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 6-month slope and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive-slope periods do not show statistically higher returns than negative-slope periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month minimum, a Sahm-rule-like recession signal activates, triggering risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger episodes since 2005, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth and INDPRO month-over-month growth signals a synchronized labor-and-production slowdown that markets underprice individually; the joint negative reading predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days more reliably than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 0 AND INDPRO MoM growth < 0 concurrently
failure_condition: Joint-negative episodes produce 20d forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term's incremental Rank IC over the single-series models is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above its 2-year rolling average), a decline in HOUST signals amplified rate sensitivity in the housing sector, foreshadowing broader demand contraction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in easing regimes the same HOUST decline carries no reliable signal because falling rates offset the demand hit.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change < 0 while FEDFUNDS > 2yr rolling average
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns following triggered HOUST declines during tightening regimes are not significantly more negative than HOUST declines during easing regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-horizon momentum tracks real-economy strength.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 3-month trend and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized inflation exceeds 4%, markets price in accelerated Fed tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as rate-hike expectations reprice discount rates sharply.
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation-print episodes show positive or flat 5d forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between elevated FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE indicates the Fed is holding policy tight even as labor markets weaken, a stagflation-like combination that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because policy space to ease lags behind deteriorating fundamentals.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor deterioration
failure_condition: 20d forward SPX returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly lower than returns during periods where only one condition holds (p > 0.05), or the interaction Rank IC increment over single factors is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate regime (FEDFUNDS below its 5-year rolling median), sustained growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in a high-rate regime the same RSAFS growth is offset by tightening credit conditions and loses predictive power.
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns conditional on strong RSAFS growth are not significantly higher in low-rate regimes than in high-rate regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.05), or overall Rank IC falls below 0.03 in low-rate subsample.
